title: Feature/batch norm
body:
Added a boolean `use_batch_norm` flag to the CNN and MLP feature extractors.
- In **NatureCNN**, each convolutional block now conditionally inserts an `nn.BatchNorm2d` layer (before the ReLU) when `use_batch_norm=True`.
- In **create_mlp**, an initial `nn.BatchNorm1d` may be applied to the inputs, and batch-norm modules can wrap each linear layer.
All defaults remain unchanged (batch norm off), and existing behavior is fully preserved.

Motivation: optional batch norm can improve training stability and convergence; claims improved PPO performance on Atari-like benchmarks.

Checklist mentions: changelog, docs, tests updated.

step_plan.summary: Add an opt-in `use_batch_norm` flag to SB3’s built-in NatureCNN and `create_mlp` utilities, inserting BatchNorm layers when enabled while keeping defaults and existing behavior unchanged. Ensure policy plumbing continues to pass `features_extractor_kwargs` correctly so users can enable BatchNorm via policy kwargs without breaking save/load or inference.

steps.operation: Add `use_batch_norm` parameter and conditionally insert `nn.BatchNorm2d` layers in each conv block (Conv2d -> BatchNorm2d -> ReLU) while preserving the exact previous architecture when disabled
steps.reason: Implements the opt-in BatchNorm behavior for the default CNN feature extractor without changing existing defaults or output shapes, ensuring stability improvements are available but not forced.
steps.side_effects: Changes the module graph when enabled; affects train/eval behavior (BatchNorm running stats) and may behave poorly with very small batch sizes; requires updating/adding tests that check layer ordering and forward pass consistency.

steps.operation: Extend `create_mlp` API with a `use_batch_norm: bool = False` (and, if required by the PR description, an input BN option) and inject `nn.BatchNorm1d` either as an initial input module and/or wrapping each linear layer in the hidden stack while keeping defaults identical
steps.reason: Provides an ergonomic, backwards-compatible way to enable BatchNorm in MLP builders used across SB3 (policy/value nets), leveraging SB3’s existing pre/post-linear module mechanism while making BatchNorm a first-class opt-in.
steps.side_effects: Signature expansion may require updating call sites only if they use positional args beyond the existing signature; BatchNorm introduces running statistics and different behavior between `.train()`/`.eval()`; must ensure output layer behavior remains unchanged and that no BN is applied when `use_batch_norm=False`.

steps.operation: Ensure `CombinedExtractor` remains compatible with the new `NatureCNN` signature (and optionally pass through BN-related kwargs if the extractor supports it) without changing default behavior
steps.reason: `CombinedExtractor` instantiates `NatureCNN` internally; if `NatureCNN` gains new kwargs, this initializer must either remain unaffected (via defaults) or explicitly handle/passthrough kwargs to avoid breaking multi-input observation policies.
steps.side_effects: Potentially alters feature extractor construction for Dict observation spaces when BN is enabled; may require adding/adjusting tests for MultiInputPolicy or dict observations if BN kwargs are intended to propagate.

steps.operation: Validate and document that `features_extractor_kwargs` safely carries the new `use_batch_norm` option (no collisions with existing auto-injected kwargs like `normalized_image`) and that it is stored for serialization
steps.reason: Policies are the user-facing entry point; the constructor is responsible for normalizing/storing `features_extractor_kwargs`. Confirming compatibility ensures users can enable BN via `policy_kwargs={'features_extractor_kwargs': {'use_batch_norm': True}}` and still save/load models reliably.
steps.side_effects: If logic is added to sanitize/merge kwargs, it can affect existing downstream policies; requires verifying that serialization of constructor parameters continues to include the new flag when provided.

steps.operation: Ensure feature extractor construction passes `features_extractor_kwargs` through unchanged so `use_batch_norm` reaches `NatureCNN` (and any other extractor) and add/adjust any typing/docstring hints to reflect the new supported kwarg
steps.reason: This is the choke point where extractors are instantiated. Verifying pass-through guarantees the new flag actually activates BatchNorm in practice when configured via policy kwargs.
steps.side_effects: If additional validation is introduced, it could reject previously tolerated kwargs; requires running existing policy construction tests and adding a targeted test that a CNN policy builds with `use_batch_norm=True` and can run a forward pass.
